text: In these spaces—one clinical, quiet, defined by glass and shadow, the other vibrant, chaotic, painted in the language of innovation—I feel caught in a dialectic. The sterile meeting room tempts introspection, almost a yearning for order or consensus. Then, in the R+D laboratory, curiosity overcomes me: the buzz of possibility, the flicker of unfamiliar ideas, the tantalizing invitation to interact, yet always from a careful distance. I am filled with wonder and slight unease—a philosopher at the threshold of the unknown, eager to understand how these spaces influence not just thought, but the trajectory of our connection to technology and each other.
